theme: duplicate clustering, source authority, and confidence scoring
purpose: Materialize one sidecar-aware shared export contract for Figure 5 and Figure 7 so singleton review and identifier-collision protection can travel together for LIMEN-000008 and LIMEN-000009 without overclaiming the current bounded 21-lineage legacy figures.
why_now: The hostile-reviewer sprint prioritizes exact claim support, denominator discipline, and package readiness over more corpus growth.
Current shared Figure 7 surfaces are already wording-synchronized on the 21 / 23 / 25 stack, so the remaining blocker is structural transport rather than another prose-only repair.

Lane-local validators and threshold boards already prove the exact omitted-row effects, but no machine-readable execution request yet names the concrete export fields, acceptance tests, and patch order for a future multistate-safe repair cycle.

current_blocking_evidence.fact: LIMEN-000008 and LIMEN-000009 are omitted from lane-011 publication-safe aggregate and source-authority-balance exports because current single-state tables flatten overlapping singleton-review and identifier-collision states.
A sidecar-aware contract would move Figure 7 from 21 to 23 lineages and Figure 5 high-confidence coverage from 15 to 17 while preserving omitted-row warnings.
Shared registry, caption, export-stub, and prose surfaces should move together only after validator-aware transport is explicit.
No active core shared Figure 7 surfaces currently drift; the blocker is the missing multistate-safe export or sidecar-aware consumption path.
Filesystem write restoration alone is insufficient because schema transport for overlapping singleton-plus-collision states is the substantive gate.

targets.current_state: 21 publication-safe lineages; confidence caps high=15, medium=0, low=6
targets.projected_state_if_repaired: 23 publication-safe lineages; high-confidence cap=17; low remains 6
questions_to_answer: Can the shared export carry both normalized lineage identity and a second join-hazard badge without collapsing to one duplicate_control_state?
Can Figure 5 retain the visible distinction between current materialized 21 and validated projected 23 rather than silently replacing the headline denominator?
Can the exported row schema preserve high-confidence eligibility while still warning that both restored rows are singleton-plus-collision cases?

targets.current_state: 21 lineages split authority_anchor=7, technical_authority=7, translation_caution_authority=1, provisional_or_limitations_only=6
targets.projected_state_if_repaired: 23 lineages split authority_anchor=8, technical_authority=8, translation_caution_authority=1, provisional_or_limitations_only=6 while still remaining below the broader 25-lineage figure-family package
questions_to_answer: Can the export preserve the exact 21 / 23 / 25 denominator-family distinction in both data and caption-ready metadata?
Can ORION remain the only translation-caution authority lineage while the two omitted T3 singleton rows are restored into the authority and technical bands?
Can the sidecar or rematerialized export expose both primary_review_state and additional_join_hazard_state so later shared surfaces do not overstate live completeness?

required_rules: Never join or count by bare local case_id; normalized_record_key is canonical and bare IDs remain provenance-only.
If a row carries both no_cluster_review_row and identifier_collision_blocker, preserve both states rather than flattening to one badge.

Do not replace the current 21-lineage materialized figure claims silently; expose 21 as current materialization and 23 as validated overlap-aware projection until or unless a true multistate-safe export becomes the canonical shared output.
Keep the broader 25-lineage figure-family duplicate/taxonomy package explicit and separate from both Figure 5 and Figure 7 denominator claims.
Translation-caution authority counts remain unchanged by this repair; do not inflate multilingual authority claims.

expected_outputs.purpose: One machine-readable row set defining fields, denominator states, patch order, and acceptance tests for future sidecar-aware export consumption.
A compact sample export or sidecar showing both omitted rows with all required multistate fields populated.
Human-readable validation note recording whether the sample satisfies the reviewer-safe 21 / 23 / 25 contract and exact band/count deltas.

acceptance_tests: Both omitted rows LIMEN-000008 and LIMEN-000009 appear with normalized_record_key plus both state fields populated.
The sample export yields Figure 7 projected bands 8 / 8 / 1 / 6 and Figure 5 projected high-confidence count 17 without changing translation-caution authority counts.
No export row uses bare local LIMEN-000008 or LIMEN-000009 as the join key.
The contract keeps 21 current materialized, 23 validated projection, and 25 broader figure-family package visibly distinct.
Patch order remains synchronized: LIMEN-VIS-005, CCR-005, exp-003, LIMEN-VIS-010, CCR-014, exp-008, publication-route bridge, dashboard spec, article architecture.
allowed_methods: Local artifacts only; no new web retrieval is required for this request.
Prefer small TSV/Markdown/JSON sidecars over shared-path promotion.
Do not patch shared denominator-bearing surfaces unless the contract and acceptance tests are satisfied in the same cycle.
Treat blocked results/dashboard writes as deployment state, not schema resolution.
paper_thesis_use: Provides a bounded handoff for the next cycle that can actually retire the most visible shard-011 denominator blocker instead of repeating drift checks.

Strengthens methods/thesis reproducibility because future writers can cite one explicit export contract for how omitted singleton-plus-collision rows are restored without overstating live figure state.
Feeds Figure 5 and Figure 7 warning badges, denominator-stack legends, and appendix-ready validator explanations.
visualization_dashboard_hook: Figure 5 / Figure 7 denominator-stack repair card with fields for current_materialized_denominator, validated_projected_denominator, broader_figure_family_denominator, omitted_row_count, authority_band_delta, and high_confidence_delta.
success_condition: A future lane produces a sample multistate-safe export or explicit sidecar contract that passes the listed tests and gives shared surfaces one synchronized, reviewer-safe path from 21 legacy counts to explicit 23 projected counts without collapsing the broader 25-lineage package into the same denominator.
next_smallest_publishability_move: Use this request to run one bounded local sidecar/export-contract cycle for Figure 5 and Figure 7 instead of reopening wording-only Figure 7 synchronization or waiting on filesystem write restoration alone.
